channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇨🇳🇳🇱 China Military Tracks Dutch Frigate in Taiwan Strait
item.description: China dispatched naval and air assets to monitor Dutch frigate HNLMS De Ruyter transiting the Taiwan Strait, the second such incident involving the warship after Beijing accused it of illegally entering Paracel Islands waters last week.

item.title: 🇷🇴🇺🇦 Maritime Drone Explodes at Romanian Black Sea Port
item.description: A Ukrainian sea drone self-detonated near an oil terminal in Romania's Constanta port without causing casualties, with Ukraine saying Russia jammed the vessel causing it to drift off course.

item.title: 🇳🇬🇿🇦 Over 1,000 Nigerians Seek Repatriation from South Africa After Xenophobic Attacks
item.description: At least 1,094 Nigerians have registered for voluntary return from South Africa following xenophobic attacks, a sharp increase from 130 previously seeking repatriation, Nigeria's government said.

item.title: 🇸🇴 Somalia Restores Order After Capital Fighting Between Government and Opposition Militias
item.description: Somali security forces restored order in two districts of Mogadishu after government troops and opposition-allied militias exchanged fire, forcing civilians to flee the capital.

item.title: 🇪🇺💰 ECB Expected to Hike Rates Next Week as Iran War Fuels Inflation Pressures
item.description: The European Central Bank is expected to raise interest rates, becoming the first major central bank to do so since the Iran conflict unleashed an energy crisis stoking inflationary pressures in the euro zone.

item.title: ⚛️ IAEA Negotiates Sixth Ceasefire to Repair Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Plant Power Line
item.description: The International Atomic Energy Agency brokered a temporary local ceasefire between Ukraine and Russia to allow repairs to a critical power supply line at the Zaporizhzhia nuclear facility, marking the sixth such negotiated pause since late last year.

item.title: 🇹🇼 Taiwan and China Coast Guards Clash Near Pratas Islands in South China Sea
item.description: Taiwan reported a second tense standoff with Chinese coast guard vessels near the strategically located Pratas Islands in two weeks, highlighting ongoing tensions in the northern South China Sea.

item.title: ⚖️ Israeli Supreme Court Overturns Ban on Red Cross Prison Visits
item.description: Israel's Supreme Court ruled that authorities must allow the International Committee of the Red Cross to visit Palestinian prisoners, overturning a ban implemented at the start of the Gaza war.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Expands Defense Exports as NATO Rearms and Alliances Shift
item.description: Two decades of state investment have transformed Turkey into a major drone and military equipment exporter, with the NATO member now looking to capitalize on Western rearmament efforts and changing security partnerships.

item.title: 🏛️ White House National Security Council Europe Chief Departs in Staff Shake-Up
item.description: The White House's top Europe expert is leaving as part of a broader National Security Council reorganization that sources say could give Secretary of State Marco Rubio tighter control over day-to-day policy making.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran fires warning missiles and drones at US warships in Gulf of Oman
item.description: Iran's navy announced it fired warning missiles and drones at US warships in the Gulf of Oman, accusing the US Navy of provocative actions. This marks a significant escalation in maritime tensions amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸 US forces board sanctioned oil tanker in Indian Ocean interdiction
item.description: US forces conducted an interdiction of a stateless sanctioned oil tanker in the Indian Ocean overnight, the Pentagon announced. The operation targets sanctions evasion networks amid heightened enforcement during the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇦🇿 Azerbaijan denies CNN report of Israeli operations against Iran from its territory
item.description: Azerbaijan rejected CNN claims that Israel was conducting operations against Iran from Azerbaijani territory, dismissing the allegations as baseless. Baku reaffirmed its commitment to regional stability and good-neighborly relations.

item.title: 🇪🇺 EU promises targeted Schengen visa restrictions for Russians after backlash
item.description: The European Union vowed to impose targeted restrictions on Schengen visas for Russian citizens after 11 European countries, led by Sweden, criticized the fragmentation of visa policies. The move addresses growing pressure to tighten entry rules.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Ukraine and Russia Exchange 185 Prisoners of War Each
item.description: Both countries completed another POW swap on Friday, exchanging 185 service personnel each. The exchange continues a series of prisoner swaps amid the ongoing conflict.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇺🇦 US House Passes Russia Sanctions and Ukraine Aid Bill
item.description: The House voted 226-195 for the Ukraine Support Act, with Republicans joining Democrats to force the vote. The bill had languished for months before reaching the floor.

item.title: 🇦🇿⚔️ Russia Accuses Ukraine of Killing Azerbaijani Crew in Ship Attack
item.description: Russia accused Ukraine of killing Azerbaijani crew members in a drone attack on two cargo vessels headed for a Russian port. Baku confirmed at least five of its citizens were killed.

item.title: 🇸🇪🇺🇦 Swedish Court Rules Seized Cargo Ship Can Go to Ukraine
item.description: A Swedish court ruled that a cargo vessel seized in the Baltic Sea can be handed to Ukraine. The ship is suspected of illegally transporting grain from Russian-occupied territory.

item.title: 🇺🇦🚢 Ukraine Strikes Cargo Ships in Sea of Azov
item.description: Ukraine said it struck five ships carrying illegal cargo in the Sea of Azov and coastal waters of Russian-occupied territory. The attacks come as Putin prepares for a key speech.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkish Inflation Exceeds Expectations Amid Regional Conflict
item.description: Turkish consumer prices rose 1.7% monthly and 32.6% annually in May, slightly above forecasts. The data highlights economic fallout from the US-Iran conflict and raises questions about monetary policy direction.

item.title: 🏛️ Senate Blocks FISA Surveillance Law Reauthorization
item.description: Seven Republicans joined Democrats to block debate on reauthorizing the expiring foreign surveillance law, dealing a blow to the program days before it "goes dark." The vote complicates efforts to extend Section 702 authorities.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran Reaffirms Hezbollah Support as Lebanon Ceasefire Falters
item.description: Iran declared continued support for Hezbollah and demanded Israeli withdrawal from southern Lebanon, complicating US efforts to broker a broader peace deal. Hezbollah rejected the ceasefire proposal while Israel said it would not withdraw troops.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Lebanon Ceasefire Hopes Dim as Fighting Continues
item.description: The US-brokered ceasefire agreement requires Hezbollah to stop firing first, but the Iran-backed group was not party to the talks and has rejected the deal. Fighting continues as diplomatic efforts face growing obstacles.

item.title: 🇦🇿 Azerbaijani citizens among dead in Sea of Azov ship attacks
item.description: Five Azerbaijani citizens were killed and three injured in attacks on two cargo vessels in the Russian-occupied Sea of Azov, raising questions about the scope of Ukrainian drone operations in the strategic waterway.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan and Indonesia begin talks on destroyer export
item.description: Japan's Defense Ministry announced an agreement with Indonesia to begin discussions on exporting Asagiri-class destroyers, marking a significant step in Japan's post-war defense export expansion.
